title: ceropegia bracteolata
intro: Ceropegia bracteolata, also known as the "Vine of Snares" or "Corkscrew Vine," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to India and Sri Lanka, where it has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are believed to promote health and well-being.
